summaries.title: Cognitive and Behavioral Development of 9-Year-Old Children After Maternal Cancer During Pregnancy: A Prospective Multicenter Cohort Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study used a neurocognitive test battery and parent-report behavioral questionnaires.
summaries.summary: A multicenter cohort study evaluating the long-term cognitive and behavioral outcomes in 9-year-old children prenatally exposed to maternal cancer and its treatment (chemotherapy, surgery, radiotherapy).
key_findings: Mean cognitive and behavioral outcomes were within normal ranges.
Gestational age at birth showed a positive association with Full Scale Intelligence Quotient (FSIQ).
No difference in FSIQ was found between treatment types or chemotherapeutic agents.
Associations were observed with preterm birth, maternal death, and maternal education.
summaries.population: 151 children (mean age 9.3 years) prenatally exposed to maternal cancer
summaries.exposure_domain: maternal cancer and treatment during pregnancy
limitations: Cross-sectional analysis at a single time point (age 9)
summaries.data_availability_statement: Multiple linear regression (Data Supplement) with GA, death of mother, and parental (maternal and paternal) education level as explanatory variables shows that GA (P = .006) and maternal education level (P = .005) remained to explain FSIQ. An overview of the reported medical problems is enlisted in the Data Supplement.

summaries.title: Evaluating Demographic Data to Improve Confidence in Equity Analytics in a Children's Hospital.
summaries.ehr_evidence: the electronic health record team standardized race and ethnicity fields... all fields are sourced from the same electronic health record field.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of demographic data collection and infrastructure at Akron Children’s Hospital to improve health equity analytics.
key_findings: Standardized race and ethnicity fields with federal definitions.
Provided 7 recommendations for aligning health equity data analytics.
summaries.study_design: Quality improvement (QI) project / data infrastructure evaluation
limitations: limitations to a seldom-populated Race 2 field
